title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Runtime Contract: Outbox Relay Confirm
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must compose database transaction ownership, relay confirms, duplicate-safe inbox effects, timeouts, and compensation state; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the outbox relay confirm increment in a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Outbox Relay Confirm · Theory Outbox Relay Confirm
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 8214 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
